item.title: Hefazat activist tells ICT of 13-month secret detention
item.description: A Hefazat-e-Islam Bangladesh activist and former madrassah teacher told the International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Thursday that he had been held for about 13 months in a series of alleged secret detention centres run by the Joint Interrogation Cell at the headquarters of the......

item.title: Court sends Actor Zaher Alvi to jail for abetting wife’s suicide
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday sent actor Zaher Alvi, also known as Md Niamat Ullah Bhuiyan, to jail after rejecting his bail petition in a case filed over allegations of abetting the suicide of his wife, Afra Ibnat Ikra....

item.title: SC stays HC bail for Dipu Moni, lawyer Suman
item.description: The chamber judge of the Appellate Division on Thursday stayed High Court orders that granted bail to former Awami League minister Dipu Moni and former independent lawmaker and Supreme Court lawyer Syed Sayedul Haque Suman in criminal cases....

item.title: Messi's father recovering from 'health issue': family
item.description:
Lionel Messi's father is recovering from an unspecified ‘health issue’, the Argentina star's family said Thursday, appealing for privacy after a flurry of recent media reports.
...

item.title: Sagar-Runi murder probe delayed for 127th time
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday deferred the submission of the investigation report in the murder case of journalist couple Sagar Sarwar and Meherun Runi for the 127th time and set July 22 as the next date....

item.title: UAE bans social media for under-15
item.description: The United Arab Emirates announced a social media ban for children under 15 on Thursday, joining a growing group of countries including Australia, Britain and Canada to take similar measures....

item.title: UN for solving push-in issues thru dialogue
item.description: The United Nations has underscored the need for resolving the ongoing push-in issues through dialogue between Bangladesh and India amid growing concerns over the humanitarian plight of people stranded along bordering areas of the two countries....

item.title: Neymar to miss Brazil's game against Haiti
item.description: Neymar will not travel with the Brazil squad for Friday's second World Cup group game against Haiti as he continues his recovery from a calf injury....

item.title: Iran president touts ‘historic’ deal with US ending war
item.description: Iran’s president Masoud Pezeshkian welcomed on Thursday what he called a ‘historic’ deal with the United States to end their war and pave the way for negotiations towards a final settlement....

item.title: Deficit may reach Tk 4t if budget fully implemented: RAPID
item.description: The budget deficit could reach nearly Tk 4 trillion if the government implements the proposed budget for 2026-27 financial year, Research and Policy Integration for Development warned on Thursday, citing expected revenue and foreign financing shortfalls......

item.title: MUSIC TEACHERS IN PRY SCHOOLS: Minister flips on Cabinet Div rejection
item.description: Education and primary and mass education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Thursday contradicted his own statement he made five days ago over the Cabinet Division declining a proposal to create music teacher positions in government primary schools....

item.title: South Africa hold Czechs to keep knockout dream alive
item.description: Teboho Mokoena's penalty kept South Africa's dream of reaching the World Cup knockout stages for the first time alive after a 1-1 draw with the Czech Republic in Atlanta on Thursday....

item.title: Bogura univ gets cabinet nod
item.description: The cabinet on Thursday approved the draft Bogura University Act, 2026, paving the way for the establishment of a full-fledged public university in the northern district, alongside a series of legal reforms aimed at tackling gambling, examination fraud and drug-related crimes....

item.title: Draft charge sheet names Atiur, 63 people, entities for BB reserve heist
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department has named 64 local and foreign people and entities, including former Bangladesh Bank governor Atiur Rahman, in its draft charge sheet in the case over the 2016 BB $101 million cyber heist....

item.title: BB RESERVE HEIST CASE: CID names Atiur among 64 people, entities in draft charge sheet
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department has named 64 local and foreign people and entities, including former Bangladesh Bank governor Atiur Rahman, in its draft charge sheet in the case over the 2016 BB $101 million cyber heist....

item.title: Important or selfish? World Cup evidence mounts against Ronaldo
item.description: Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe, Erling Haaland and Harry Kane all made blistering starts to the World Cup. And then there was Cristiano Ronaldo....

item.title: Rural consumers must not bear the brunt
item.description: THE prolonged power outages sweeping across rural areas are a stark reminder of the deep inequalities embedded in the electricity distribution system. Villages now reportedly receive electricity for only six to 10 hours a day. This raises serious questions about fairness, governance and development priorities as urban centres consume vast amounts of power for air......

item.title: Increased education budget requires effective use
item.description: THE allocation for education in the national budget proposed for the 2026–27 financial year has been increased to 2 per cent of the estimated gross domestic product for the financial year. Given the Tk 68.30 trillion gross domestic product estimated for the financial year, the allocation for the education sector stands at Tk 1.36 trillion, up by 56.65 per cent from Tk 872.06......

item.title: Post-LDC trade challenge
item.description: BANGLADESH stands less than six months away from one of the most significant milestones in its economic history. In November 2026, the country is set to graduate from the Least Developed Country category, marking the culmination of decades of progress in industrialisation, poverty reduction and export growth. Yet graduation also marks the end of a period......

item.title: Managing soil acidity
item.description: WHILE Bangladesh has successfully increased food production over the past five decades, a silent threat beneath the soil is gradually reducing the productivity of millions of hectares of farmland. Unless this challenge is addressed, future gains in crop production, fertiliser efficiency and farm profitability may become increasingly difficult to achieve......

item.title: Foreign investment needs credible state
item.description: OVER three decades, Bangladesh has emerged as one of South Asia’s most compelling economic success stories. Sustained GDP growth, declining poverty, expanding infrastructure, a growing manufacturing base and rising export earnings have transformed the economic landscape. Yet, despite the achievements, one question continues to challenge policy-makers and......

item.title: Cracking classroom code
item.description: EDUCATION has long been described as the backbone of national development, yet the way it is delivered in most classrooms still reflects an older world. In an era defined by rapid technological change, global interdependence and shifting labour markets, the traditional model of education where teachers speak and students silently receive feels increasingly out of......

item.title: 'Big-game' Bellingham shows his worth for England
item.description: Jude Bellingham arrived at the World Cup facing a battle to secure a place in England's starting line-up, but emphatically ended any debate with a barnstorming display against Croatia....

item.title: Tuchel 1 photographers 0 after World Cup moment 'ruined'
item.description: Thomas Tuchel said a ‘special moment was ruined’ and FIFA have now reportedly acted to the England coach being swarmed by photographers during the national anthems ahead of the World Cup clash with Croatia....

item.title: Subs send Swiss to rout of Bosnia
item.description:
Super subs Johan Manzambi and Ruben Vargas fired Switzerland to a 4-1 win over 10-man Bosnia-Herzegovina in a late rush of goals in Los Angeles on Thursday.
...

item.title: BIDA focuses on local investors amid energy crisis, weak FDI
FICCI calls for digital tax ecosystem
item.description: The Foreign Investors’ Chamber of Commerce and Industry on Thursday urged the government to implement sweeping structural reforms, including automated tax administration and a broadened tax base to maintain a competitive investment climate ahead of Bangladesh’s LDC graduation......

item.description: Customs House Dhaka on Thursday issued a special e-auction notice inviting bids for the sale of various goods stored at the Airfreight Unit, Express Service Unit and state warehouses under Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport......

item.title: Modi reports US-India trade progress
item.description: Indian prime minister Narendra Modi said there had been ‘significant progress’ in long-running talks with Washington for a trade deal, according to a statement issued Thursday after his meeting with president Donald Trump......

item.title: BSEC, BAPLC stress quality companies at stocks
item.description: The Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission and the Bangladesh Association of Publicly Listed Companies (BAPLC) on Thursday held a strategic meeting to discuss ways to further strengthen the country’s capital market through improved listings and long-term financing mechanisms......

item.title: BRAC Bank customers to enjoy multibrand WORKSHOP benefits
item.description: BRAC Bank has recently entered into a partnership with multibrand WORKSHOP ltd, one of the country’s leading premium automobile service centres, to offer exclusive benefits to its premium banking customers and selected credit cardholders, said a press release......

item.title: Oil falls further on Mideast deal
item.description: Oil prices tumbled further Thursday after US president Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart signed off on a deal to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz to tanker and cargo traffic......

item.title: US Fed chair vows reforms as central bank signals rate hikes on horizon
item.description: US Federal Reserve chair Kevin Warsh on Wednesday vowed wide-ranging reforms at the central bank, as its rate-setting committee held rates steady but projected a rate hike by year-end to counter surging inflation......

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI presales to begin June 25
item.description: Pre-sales of Grand Theft Auto VI, the feverishly anticipated video game release, will begin on June 25, Rockstar Games said on Thursday......

item.title: Apple CEO says AI boom makes price increases ‘unavoidable’
item.description: Outgoing Apple CEO Tim Cook warned that demand for memory chips from the artificial intelligence boom will force the tech giant to increase the prices of its products, according to an interview with the Wall Street Journal published Wednesday......

item.title: US gasoline prices drop below $4 per gallon
item.description: US consumers saw some relief at the fuel pump on Thursday, as the average price of a gallon of regular gasoline fell below $4 for the first time since March, according to the AAA motor club......

item.title: IBFB hails budget, hopes economic recovery
item.description: International Business Forum of Bangladesh (IBFB) has described the proposed budget for FY 2026-27 as a positive and pragmatic roadmap aimed at driving economic recovery, expanding investment, generating employment, and advancing long-term structural reforms......

item.title: Stocks witness recovery
item.description: Stocks on Thursday witnessed a recovery trading session, supported by renewed investor interest in selective blue-chip and fundamentally strong shares amid improving sentiment and easing external uncertainties......

item.title: Intex Bangladesh 2026 begins
item.description: South Asia’s premier international textile sourcing platform, Intex Bangladesh 2026, officially opened on Thursday at the International Convention City Bashundhara (ICCB), Dhaka......

item.title: Pragati Insurance declares 30pc dividends
item.description: Shareholders of Pragati Insurance PLC approved 27 per cent cash and 3 per cent stock dividend for the year 2025 during its 40th annual general meeting held on Thursday through digital platform......

item.description: Bangladesh Bank executive director Mohammad Zahir Hussain, the authorised representative for Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC’s board affairs, exchanged views with the officials and employees of the bank on Wednesday......

item.description: Southeast Bank PLC has recently observed World Environment Day 2026, with theme ‘Green Banking for a Resilient Bangladesh,’ through a series of nationwide programmes aimed at promoting environmental awareness and conservation......

item.title: Kurigram rail services resume after 12 hours
item.description: Rail communication between Kurigram and the rest of the country resumed on Thursday after remaining suspended for around 12 hours when a sand-laden truck overturned on a railway track in Rajarhat upazila....

item.description: Over 100 workers of a readymade garment factory in Kaliakair upazila of Gazipur district fell sick on Thursday morning, reportedly after drinking water at the factory....

item.description: Community Bank Bangladesh PLC has recently entered into an agreement with Flora Systems Limited to implement an advanced Islamic Core Banking Solution, said a press release......

item.description: Bangladesh and Azerbaijan on Thursday agreed to open resident diplomatic missions in each other’s capitals as the two countries move to deepen bilateral relations and expand diplomatic engagement....

item.description: Two construction workers were killed after falling from an under-construction multi-storey building in Mymensingh city Thursday afternoon....

item.description: Newly appointed chief of the International Organization for Migration in Bangladesh Laura Tomm Bonde on Thursday reaffirmed IOM’s readiness to support Bangladesh in enhancing regular migration pathways and advancing whole-of-government and......

item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard in an operation seized 20kg of hemp at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Wednesday....

item.description: Ambassador and head of the European Union delegation to Bangladesh Michael Miller on Thursday said the European Union is committed to supporting Rohingyas and the communities hosting them in Bangladesh....

item.description: Prime Bank PLC has retained the highest credit ratings of AAA for long-term and ST-1 for short-term creditworthiness from Credit Rating Agency of Bangladesh Limited......

item.description: Prime minister’s information and broadcasting adviser Zahed Ur Rahman on Thursday said that consensus was reached among stakeholders on two important issues at the high-level workshop on formulating the framework for a national media commission....

item.description: The police on Thursday detained a pharmacy owner in Dhaka’s Ashulia on allegations of raping an 11-year-old girl who had gone to buy medicine....

item.title: Testifying cop links former SP’s ‘crossfire’ orders to AL leader Hasanat’s pressure
item.description: A police official told the International Crimes Tribunal on Thursday that a former superintendent of police in Barishal had ordered police officials to kill two Bangladesh Nationalist Party activists in a reported ‘crossfire’ in February 2015, but he had refused to carry out the order....

item.description: Army chief General Waker-Uz-Zaman emphasised the critical role of a modern, efficient, and patriotic army in safeguarding the independence and sovereignty of the nation....

item.description: At least five people were killed by lightning strikes in Sylhet and Netrakona districts on Thursday....

item.title: Four arrested over AL flash processions in Dhaka
item.description: The police on Thursday morning arrested four suspected activists of the Awami League, the activities of which are now banned, and its affiliated organisations for their alleged involvement in exploding crude bombs during a flash procession in the capital’s Mohakhali area....

item.title: Woman killed in crocodile attack
item.description: A woman fisherman was killed in a crocodile attack while collecting fish fry in Sundarban’ Joymoni area of Mongla in Bagerhat Wednesday afternoon....

item.title: Budget allocation to fund ‘decent job’ for women demanded
item.description: Bangladesh Mahila Parishad on Thursday demanded that the government increase budget allocations for ‘decent jobs’ to support gender equality and women’s empowerment in the 2026-27 financial year....

item.title: Ex-Somoy TV MD Jobaer denied bail, sent to jail in fraud case
item.description: A Dhaka court sent Ahmed Jobaer, former managing director of Somoy Television, to jail on Thursday after rejecting his bail petition in a fraud case....

item.description: The Dhaka University of Engineering and Technology held an event titled Annual Paper Meet, highlighting the results, innovations and future prospects of the research projects approved for the 2025-2026 financial year for teachers, at DUET in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.description: The master of business administration and executive MBA programmes of North South University organised a business strategy dialogue, featuring Unilever Bangladesh Limited’s chief executive officer and managing director Ruhul Quddus Khan, at NSU in Dhaka on Tuesday....

item.description: The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell of Independent University, Bangladesh has recently organised a two-day international conference titled International Conference on Innovation, Excellence and Quality for Transformation in Higher Education at IUB in Dhaka....

item.description: A five-day training programme titled Entrepreneurship Development was concluded at the Begum Rokeya University, Rangpur on Wednesday....

item.title: 2 in 3 rural adolescent girls face menstrual disorders
item.description: Nearly two in three adolescent girls in rural Bangladesh suffer menstrual disorders with painful periods, disrupting their daily lives and forcing many to miss school, according to a study conducted by International Centre for Diarrhoeal Diseases Research, Bangladesh....
